design-brief.content:
First-run onboarding for a bookkeeping web app used by solo founders and tiny teams. One page: welcome, 4-step checklist (connect bank, categorize expenses, invite accountant, set tax year), inline tips, and a persistent 'resume later' secondary action.

Tone: calm, encouraging, plain language. Visual: light background, soft green accent for completed steps, clear disabled vs active states.

research-context.content: Users abandon when they feel rushed; progressive disclosure + saved progress reduces drop-off.

objectives-metrics.content: Increase completion of step 1 and 2 in first session. Success: users understand what 'connect bank' entails before clicking.

design-constraints.content: Static prototype only. Simulate checklist state with simple JS or CSS :checked patterns; no real OAuth.

strategy.name: Progressive disclosure checklist
strategy.hypothesis: Revealing detail for only the active step (collapsed copy for other steps) will reduce cognitive load versus showing all step instructions expanded at once.
strategy.rationale: Chunking limits parallel tasks; users complete serially when overwhelmed.
strategy.measurements: Rubric: primary CTA visible without scroll on mobile; step states visually distinct; help text tied to active step only.
